title: ophrys araneola subsp. illyrica
intro: Ophrys araneola subsp. illyrica, commonly known as the Spider Orchid, is a type of terrestrial orchid that belongs to the Ophrys genus. This plant is known for its unique appearance, with a brownish-yellow color and a distinctive spider-like shape, which is thought to mimic the appearance of an insect to attract pollinators. The medicinal properties of the Spider Orchid include its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various health issues. The health benefits of the plant include its ability to reduce inflammation, soothe digestive issues, and act as a natural antiseptic, and it can be prepared as a tea or tincture for herbal use.
